name: Vigil Security Camera (Pi Zero 2 W + C920, headless)
description: Pi OS Lite 64-bit baked into a FULLY HEADLESS security camera — no display, no X, no pygame/SDL, no HDMI/LCD. Captures the Logitech C920, runs frame-diff motion detection, serves a full-rate LAN MJPEG stream (:8090/stream), and (once VIGIL_PASSWORD is set) uploads PRIVATE owner-only snapshots + motion events to sinsera.co/vigil. opencv-python-headless + numpy via pip; the daemon runs as the 'vigil' systemd service (Restart=always). Same plumbing as Mirror Loop, stripped to pure surveillance and hardened (private bucket, signed-URL only).

preconfigured.cloud: PRIVATE owner-only frames + motion events to sinsera.co/vigil; VIGIL_PASSWORD BLANK by default (fill over SSH to enable)
preconfigured.env: /opt/vigil/.env — anon key + email baked; VIGIL_PASSWORD blank
preconfigured.tuning: 640x480@15 capture, cloud 2fps (4 on motion), JPEG q75, frame-diff motion

notes: UNVERIFIED ON HARDWARE — built without a Zero 2 W to test on; expect on-Pi tuning (C920 v4l2 index, motion threshold, fps).
Private by design: cloud frames go to a private Supabase bucket (owner RLS, signed URLs only). VIGIL_PASSWORD blank → cloud off, LAN MJPEG still works.
Headless: no HDMI/LCD output at all — it's a camera, not a display.
